title: The White Hole

jokes.text: I fixed my hearing aid.
jokes.setup: Professor announces good news to everyone
jokes.punchline: The 'good news' is just fixing his hearing aid - anticlimactic
jokes.explanation: Classic Professor Farnsworth - builds up mundane things as important. Good character integration and economy.

jokes.text: In my day, sound didn't travel through space.
jokes.setup: Professor complaining about kids these days
jokes.punchline: His complaint is about a basic law of physics being violated
jokes.explanation: Brilliant sci-fi comedy - treating impossible sci-fi premise as generational complaint. Perfect for Professor character.

jokes.text: It's not 'Walking on Sunshine'. It's not 'Foggy Mountain Breakdown'. What else is there?
jokes.setup: Professor trying to identify familiar melody
jokes.punchline: Implies only two songs exist in the universe
jokes.explanation: Absurd reductionism that fits the Professor's limited perspective. Good specificity in song choices.

jokes.text: His name is shazam.
jokes.setup: Professor mentions having a friend who identifies melodies
jokes.punchline: His 'friend' is the Shazam app - misunderstanding technology as personal
jokes.explanation: Perfect Professor moment - anthropomorphizing technology. Great economy and character fit.

jokes.text: Your singing is awful. I can't take it anymore.
jokes.setup: Professor asks Shazam to identify the song
jokes.punchline: Shazam responds as if it's a person insulting his singing
jokes.explanation: Excellent payoff to the Shazam setup. AI getting fed up with user is relatable and well-executed.

jokes.text: Why am I the only one whining?
jokes.setup: Professor complaining about the music
jokes.punchline: Self-aware that he's the only one complaining, but continues anyway
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Professor's self-awareness but inability to change.

jokes.text: Why don't they just use a thoughtspike like normal people?
jokes.setup: Aliens communicating through musical tones
jokes.punchline: In this universe, 'thoughtspike' is the normal communication method
jokes.explanation: Good sci-fi worldbuilding humor - treating bizarre future tech as mundane.

jokes.text: Isn't it obvious? ... Uh, no. ... Drat, I was hoping it was.
jokes.setup: Bender claims the meaning is obvious
jokes.punchline: When pressed, admits he was just hoping it was obvious
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender moment - fake confidence followed by honest admission. Great structure and economy.

jokes.text: That's a hell of a slow ship.
jokes.setup: Ship will reach Earth in two weeks
jokes.punchline: Casual dismissal of interstellar travel time as 'slow'
jokes.explanation: Good sci-fi perspective joke. Bender's casual dismissal of impossible travel times.

jokes.text: I always forget that he can talk.
jokes.setup: Nibbler speaks about his mission
jokes.punchline: Fry treats Nibbler's intelligence as forgettable detail
jokes.explanation: Good callback to series lore. Fry's casual dismissal of major revelation fits his character.

jokes.text: Like Kaboom cereal. And my family.
jokes.setup: Fry saying memory is gone forever
jokes.punchline: Equates his dead family with discontinued cereal
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy. The casual juxtaposition of family tragedy with cereal is perfectly Fry-like and unexpectedly poignant.

jokes.text: Will I have pants on in this dream? ... Only if you wear these dream pants. ... Hey, this is a diaper.
jokes.setup: Fry asks reasonable question about dream pants
jokes.punchline: Professor's solution is a diaper disguised as 'dream pants'
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor with practical punchline. Professor's casual deception is in character.

jokes.text: Almost. Can I have some milk and cookies first? ... Yes. In syringe form.
jokes.setup: Fry requests bedtime comfort food
jokes.punchline: Professor accommodates but in unnecessarily medical way
jokes.explanation: Good Professor moment - over-engineering simple requests. Visual absurdity works well.

jokes.text: Not even close, you tone-deaf moron. Now get out of bed and start dreaming.
jokes.setup: Fry guesses his alarm clock is the music
jokes.punchline: Professor's harsh dismissal followed by contradictory command
jokes.explanation: Good Professor crankiness. The contradiction of 'get out of bed and start dreaming' adds absurdity.

jokes.text: Old? Don't call me old. A lot of young people have false teeth.
jokes.setup: Fry calls his girlfriend from the past 'old'
jokes.punchline: Her defensive response reveals she has false teeth
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment. The false teeth detail adds specificity to the defensive response.

jokes.text: Don't worry about it, Michelle. You're just a dream. In which case, it wouldn't really be cheating on Leela if we were to...
jokes.setup: Fry reassures Michelle she's just a dream
jokes.punchline: Immediately tries to use dream logic to justify cheating
jokes.explanation: Good Fry moment showing his poor decision-making and rationalization skills. Cringe element works.

jokes.text: Move it along!
jokes.setup: Fry about to get intimate with dream girlfriend
jokes.punchline: Professor's impatient interruption from outside the dream
jokes.explanation: Good timing-based humor. Professor's business-like interruption of intimate moment.

jokes.text: Man, I always wanted to ride in that stroller. But nope.
jokes.setup: Adult Fry sees baby stroller in his past
jokes.punchline: Adult regret about never riding in a stroller
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Fry's childish desires and disappointments. Absurd but relatable.

jokes.text: Yo, Cosmic F. What up? Hey, my old break dancing crew.
jokes.setup: Fry encounters people from his past
jokes.punchline: 'Cosmic F' was apparently Fry's breakdancing name
jokes.explanation: Nice character detail - 'Cosmic F' as Fry's breakdancing alias fits his dorky attempt to be cool in the 90s.

jokes.text: Yo, Noticeably F.A.T., drop me that one beat that I'm having a hard time remembering.
jokes.setup: Fry addressing his break dancing crew member
jokes.punchline: The name 'Noticeably F.A.T.' - both descriptive and acronym
jokes.explanation: Clever name that works as both description and acronym. Good period detail for late 90s.

jokes.text: Damn, don't nobody like beat boxing no more? I'ma go take that job offer at Lehman Brothers.
jokes.setup: Breakdancer disappointed about beat boxing's declining popularity
jokes.punchline: References Lehman Brothers job offer (knowing it will collapse in 2008)
jokes.explanation: Excellent historical irony joke. The casual mention of Lehman Brothers from 1999 perspective is brilliant dark comedy for audiences who know the 2008 collapse.

jokes.text: Whoa. This place is a lot more nude than I expected.
jokes.setup: Fry enters neighborhood strip club
jokes.punchline: Surprised by nudity at strip club - shows his naivety
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Fry's innocent surprise at obvious things. Fits his naive character.

jokes.text: You never went in there, so you can't remember it. ... Ah, rats. I wanted lap-dance nachos.
jokes.setup: Professor explains dream logic limitations
jokes.punchline: Fry's disappointment is about missing 'lap-dance nachos'
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurd concept. 'Lap-dance nachos' is a perfectly ridiculous strip club food item that sounds plausible.

jokes.text: Ah, rats. I wanted lap-dance nachos.
jokes.setup: Fry disappointed he can't explore the strip club
jokes.punchline: Was specifically hoping for 'lap-dance nachos'
jokes.explanation: 'Lap-dance nachos' is a wonderfully absurd combination that perfectly captures Fry's simple pleasures and the show's bizarre worldbuilding.

jokes.text: No, you need a turkey dinner. You have to get back to dreamland.
jokes.setup: Fry wants coffee to stay awake
jokes.punchline: Professor prescribes turkey dinner for drowsiness
jokes.explanation: Good Professor logic - treating tryptophan drowsiness as medical prescription. Scientific but absurd.

jokes.text: Exactly. And you've been asleep for 13 and a half days.
jokes.setup: Fry thinks they have two weeks before alien ship arrives
jokes.punchline: Reveals most of that time has already passed while he slept
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and timing. The revelation that time is almost up raises stakes effectively.

jokes.text: Ow! Suing! Ow!
jokes.setup: Fry gets turkey injection presumably
jokes.punchline: His immediate threat to sue while still being injured
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy with modern litigious mindset. Economy is good but concept is basic.

jokes.text: Aw, this never happens, I swear.
jokes.setup: Fry apparently can't fall asleep for the dream
jokes.punchline: Phrase commonly associated with erectile dysfunction
jokes.explanation: Basic innuendo joke. Works contextually but relies on cheap sexual reference.

jokes.text: Can't you just dissect my brain? ... With the building shaking like this? No chance.
jokes.setup: Fry suggests brain dissection as alternative to dreaming
jokes.punchline: Professor's concern is about building shaking, not the brain dissection
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy. Fry casually suggesting brain dissection and Professor treating it as viable but impractical due to circumstances.

jokes.text: There's not one thing about this place that I missed.
jokes.setup: Fry claims he didn't miss his old house
jokes.punchline: Immediately followed by him reuniting emotionally with his dog
jokes.explanation: Good ironic setup that's immediately contradicted. Shows Fry's emotional defenses.

jokes.text: Maybe I just convinced myself I hated it because I knew I could never come back.
jokes.setup: Fry realizing he does miss his past
jokes.punchline: Self-aware psychological insight about defensive coping mechanism
jokes.explanation: Excellent character development moment. Rare moment of genuine psychological insight from Fry that rings very true.

jokes.text: By which I mean you.
jokes.setup: Yancy complains about dog tracking stink in
jokes.punchline: Clarifies he means Fry is the source of stink
jokes.explanation: Basic sibling insult structure. Good economy but predictable punchline.

jokes.text: Attaboy, Yancy. Gorbachev that forehead up good.
jokes.setup: Dad encouraging Yancy somehow
jokes.punchline: Using 'Gorbachev' as verb referring to forehead birthmark
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay using Gorbachev's distinctive birthmark as verb. Creative and period-appropriate.

jokes.text: Nobody. I'm watching the Charlie Brown special.
jokes.setup: Fry asks who's playing in the football game
jokes.punchline: Mom reveals she's watching animated special, not real sports
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection joke. Sets up sports expectation then reveals cartoon special.

jokes.text: Just let me unplug it before you...
jokes.setup: Mom offers to let Fry lick the beaters
jokes.punchline: She tries to unplug the mixer too late, implying Fry got hurt
jokes.explanation: Classic kitchen slapstick - the too-late safety warning implies painful but minor injury.

jokes.text: Just let me unplug it before you... Mmm.
jokes.setup: Mom warns Fry about unplugging beaters
jokes.punchline: Fry gets hit by beaters before she can finish warning
jokes.explanation: Classic physical comedy timing. Predictable but well-executed with good interrupted warning structure.

jokes.text: Dear God. He's not looking for the melody at all. He's enjoying himself.
jokes.setup: Professor watching Fry's dream progress
jokes.punchline: Horrified realization that Fry is having fun instead of working
jokes.explanation: Good Professor moment treating happiness as problematic. Nice contrast between mission urgency and human emotion.

jokes.text: Well, no, not in a dream. Lots of weird stuff could happen.
jokes.setup: Fry asks if hearing Professor's voice seems strange
jokes.punchline: Dream logic makes outside voices seem normal
jokes.explanation: Good meta joke about dream logic. Characters accepting impossible things as normal in dreams.

jokes.text: Damn it, Fry. Stop wasting time with your loved ones and find the source of the music.
jokes.setup: Fry enjoying time with family
jokes.punchline: Professor frames family time as 'wasting time with loved ones'
jokes.explanation: Excellent Professor moment. The phrase 'wasting time with your loved ones' perfectly captures his cold scientific priorities over human emotion.

jokes.text: You mean before the Y2K turns our computers and waffle irons against us?
jokes.setup: Discussion about New Year's Eve plans
jokes.punchline: Y2K fear extended to absurd appliances like waffle irons
jokes.explanation: Good period humor. Captures Y2K hysteria while extending it absurdly to kitchen appliances.

jokes.text: How are you gonna pay off your debt to the Columbia House Record Club if you keep losing jobs?
jokes.setup: Yancy warning Fry about getting fired
jokes.punchline: Specific reference to Columbia House Record Club debt
jokes.explanation: Good period-specific reference. Columbia House was a real predatory music club that many people fell into debt with.

jokes.text: Mom? You know Leela? ... Nope.
jokes.setup: Mom responds to Leela as if she knows her
jokes.punchline: Casually denies knowing her despite just interacting
jokes.explanation: Good dream logic joke. Characters behaving inconsistently but treating it as normal.

jokes.text: That's as close as I'm gonna get.
jokes.setup: Leela points out that dream mom isn't really his mother
jokes.punchline: Fry accepts this sad limitation as his only option
jokes.explanation: Poignant character moment disguised as humor - Fry's acceptance of his permanent loss is both funny and heartbreaking.

jokes.text: The world's ending and your dream pants need changing.
jokes.setup: Leela explaining urgency to Fry
jokes.punchline: Juxtaposes apocalyptic urgency with diaper change
jokes.explanation: Excellent juxtaposition of cosmic stakes with bathroom humor. Great economy and callback to earlier diaper joke.

jokes.text: Oh, my God, is that a Captain Crunch casserole?
jokes.setup: The crew enters Fry's dream house
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg is immediately distracted by the absurd food
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Zoidberg's food obsession immediately kicks in upon seeing the bizarre casserole.

jokes.text: Oh, my God, is that a Captain Crunch casserole?
jokes.setup: Zoidberg enters Fry's house on urgent mission
jokes.punchline: Immediately distracted by unusual food item
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zoidberg moment. His food obsession overriding mission urgency is completely in character.

jokes.text: Manwiches?
jokes.setup: Professor telling everyone to stop eating
jokes.punchline: Hermes also distracted by food despite the urgency
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of food distraction theme. One-word delivery shows character priorities.

jokes.text: The waffle iron! It's turned against us!
jokes.setup: Earlier Y2K fears about appliances
jokes.punchline: Y2K fear seemingly coming true with attacking waffle iron
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback to earlier Y2K joke. Makes the absurd fear seem prophetic and adds visual comedy.

jokes.text: Good God, it's Richard Nixon, our greatest President!
jokes.setup: Nixon appears in the dream
jokes.punchline: Professor's over-the-top praise of historically controversial president
jokes.explanation: Good Professor moment showing his skewed historical perspective. Nixon as 'greatest' is absurd but fits character.

jokes.text: That's right, daddy-o, and I need to take your hippie son on a far-out musical quest.
jokes.setup: Nixon appearing as authority figure
jokes.punchline: Nixon using 1960s hippie slang inappropriately
jokes.explanation: Excellent character humor. Nixon using counterculture language he famously opposed is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Get out of my house, you commie.
jokes.setup: Nixon trying to take Fry away
jokes.punchline: Dad calls the famously anti-communist Nixon a communist
jokes.explanation: Good ironic insult. Calling anti-communist Nixon a communist shows dad's protective instincts over political accuracy.

jokes.text: Let's go already.
jokes.setup: Nixon offering to sing a recognizable tune
jokes.punchline: His 'song' is just impatient spoken words to a tune
jokes.explanation: Good Nixon characterization. His 'helpful' song is just impatient demands, showing his personality.

jokes.text: Your mom died hundreds of years ago. Get over it!
jokes.setup: Fry expressing grief about leaving his mother
jokes.punchline: Professor's brutal, factual dismissal of grief
jokes.explanation: Excellent Professor moment. His complete lack of emotional sensitivity delivered with scientific matter-of-factness is perfectly in character and darkly funny.

jokes.text: You stink, loser.
jokes.setup: Fry playing arcade game poorly
jokes.punchline: Game insults him personally beyond just losing
jokes.explanation: Basic video game insult humor. The personal nature adds slight absurdity but it's a simple concept.

jokes.text: that's just the sound the game makes when you get the all-time lowest score.
jokes.setup: They investigate a sound at the arcade
jokes.punchline: It's just Fry being historically bad at video games
jokes.explanation: Good character detail - Fry's video game incompetence is both pathetic and endearing, fitting his loser persona.

jokes.text: Shrink down and get in my pocket.
jokes.setup: Fry realizing this is his dream so he can control it
jokes.punchline: Uses dream power for impractical pet transportation
jokes.explanation: Good dream logic humor. Fry using ultimate power for mundane pet convenience is very in character.

jokes.text: Dream up some kind of luxury vehicle so we can ride in style.
jokes.setup: Everyone needs transportation in the dream
jokes.punchline: Bender immediately tries to exploit dream logic for luxury
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender moment. Immediately thinking of how to use the situation for personal luxury is completely in character.

jokes.text: It's called a car. And no.
jokes.setup: Someone asks if the non-hovering hover car is making music
jokes.punchline: Leela's matter-of-fact correction and dismissal
jokes.explanation: Good Leela moment. Her practical, no-nonsense correction of future people not recognizing 20th century car.

jokes.text: Hey, you hussy! You can't dump Fry! That's my job!
jokes.setup: Michelle breaking up with Fry in the past
jokes.punchline: Leela claiming exclusive rights to dump Fry
jokes.explanation: Excellent Leela moment showing her possessive but ambivalent feelings about Fry. Good relationship dynamic humor.

jokes.text: I.C. Wiener? Aw, crud. Every time!
jokes.setup: Fry delivering pizza to address
jokes.punchline: Classic prank call name that sounds like 'Icy Wiener'
jokes.explanation: Important series callback to the prank call that led to Fry's freezing. Low originality as prank call joke but high series significance.

jokes.text: Like a delicious frozen Charleston Chew!
jokes.setup: Describing how the dream world is cracking apart
jokes.punchline: Compares cosmic destruction to candy bar
jokes.explanation: Good Professor moment. His tendency to relate everything to food/candy even during crisis is characteristically odd.

jokes.text: Amy, I'm deeply disappointed in you.
jokes.setup: Amy asks if the noisemaker was the source of music
jokes.punchline: Professor uses same phrase Fry used before blowing noisemaker
jokes.explanation: Good callback setup for the noisemaker reveal. Creates parallel between past and present disappointment.

jokes.text: I blew my noisemaker sarcastically to underscore my deep disappointment with life.
jokes.setup: Fry recalls his final action before freezing
jokes.punchline: He describes a simple action with overwrought emotional context
jokes.explanation: Excellent character voice - the pretentious description of a sarcastic party favor toot perfectly captures Fry's mix of self-awareness and melodrama.

jokes.text: No pressure.
jokes.setup: Explaining that wrong notes mean everyone dies
jokes.punchline: Casual 'no pressure' after describing apocalyptic stakes
jokes.explanation: Standard sarcastic understatement. Works contextually but is a very common joke structure.

jokes.text: Hey, you remember where we parked?
jokes.setup: The Nibblonians are drunk after celebrating
jokes.punchline: Even advanced aliens have the universal drunk problem of forgetting parking
jokes.explanation: Relatable humor - advanced aliens experiencing the mundane drunk problem of lost parking makes them more human.

jokes.text: Give me that. You're in no condition to fly. ... Bye-bye, keys.
jokes.setup: Drunk Nibbler trying to drive spaceship
jokes.punchline: Keys get thrown away during drunk argument
jokes.explanation: Standard drunk driving prevention joke with sci-fi twist. The key-throwing adds physical comedy element.

jokes.text: Although the cassette player's been stolen and the battery's dead.
jokes.setup: Finding the alien ship after cosmic quest
jokes.punchline: Ship has typical earthly car problems despite being alien tech
jokes.explanation: Excellent mundane humor. Advanced alien spaceship suffering from common car theft and dead battery is perfect anticlimax.

jokes.text: I'll push him off the roof.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg can't stand to see Fry sad
jokes.punchline: Bender's solution is to kill Fry
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender moment. His compassionate solution involving murder is completely in character. Great economy and dark humor.

jokes.text: You want some crackers and a slice of hat?
jokes.setup: Fry's mother offers him snacks in the dream
jokes.punchline: She offers 'a slice of hat' as if it's normal food
jokes.explanation: Surreal dream logic - 'slice of hat' treated as a normal food option creates absurd domestic comedy.

jokes.text: Sure, honey, you're the most important thing in my life once this game is over.
jokes.setup: Fry asking for his mom's attention
jokes.punchline: Mom prioritizes TV game over son but phrases it sweetly
jokes.explanation: Good parental humor. The conditional love based on TV programming is relatable and well-structured.

jokes.text: I never saw that game. It happened after I got frozen. How can I be dreaming it in such detail?
jokes.setup: Fry watching detailed Rose Bowl game in dream
jokes.punchline: Realizes he couldn't dream something he never experienced
jokes.explanation: Excellent setup for the major revelation. Fry's logical deduction about dream limitations creates perfect story turn.

jokes.text: Because this isn't your dream. It's your mother's dream.
jokes.setup: Fry's confusion about impossible dream details
jokes.punchline: Reveals this is actually his deceased mother's dream
jokes.explanation: Masterful revelation that reframes the entire episode. The emotional weight and narrative surprise make this exceptional writing. Transforms comedy episode into deeply moving story.

tmdb_overview: The crew embarks on an epic ten-million-year journey to witness the birth of a new universe.
